action.reasoning: Observation: CLIP ViT added meaningful diversity and raised the ensemble to 0.8173; current code already supports resnet50 in model.py and train_clip.py is a generic pretrained-backbone trainer with compatible checkpoint format. Analysis: a ResNet50 ImageNet-pretrained model is likely weaker standalone than ConvNeXt/CLIP but architecturally diverse, so even a modestly accurate checkpoint may improve an ensemble. This is a safe incremental run under the <20 epoch rule and within time. Decision: train an 8-epoch ResNet50 EMA checkpoint using the existing validated trainer, create a 5-model ensemble with a modest ResNet weight, and submit evaluation.
action.command: python train_clip.py --arch resnet50 --out checkpoints/resnet8.pt --epochs 8 --batch_size 128 --lr_backbone 5e-5 --lr_head 1e-3 --mixup 0.4 --smoothing 0.12 --seed 321 && python -c "import torch; torch.save({'ensemble':['ema16.pt','ema8.pt','pseudo8.pt','clip6.pt','resnet8.pt'], 'weights':[0.32,0.21,0.17,0.20,0.10]}, 'checkpoints/ensemble_resnet_v1.pt'); print('wrote checkpoints/ensemble_resnet_v1.pt')"
